1	//go:build linux && amd64
2	
3	package turbostat
4	
5	import (
6		"flag"
7		"fmt"
8		"io"
9		"os"
10		"path/filepath"
11		"testing"
12		"time"
13	
14		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
15	
16		"github.com/influxdata/telegraf"
17		"github.com/influxdata/telegraf/config"
18		"github.com/influxdata/telegraf/metric"
19		"github.com/influxdata/telegraf/plugins/parsers/influx"
20		"github.com/influxdata/telegraf/testutil"
21	)
22	
23	func TestCreateColumn(t *testing.T) {
24		tests := []struct {
25			in  string
26			out column
27		}{
28			{in: "Core", out: column{name: "core", isTag: true}},
29			{in: "Busy%", out: column{name: "busy_percent"}},
30			{in: "Bzy_MHz", out: column{name: "busy_frequency_mhz"}},
31			{in: "C3-", out: column{name: "c3_minus"}},
32			{in: "CoreThr", out: column{name: "core_throttle"}},
33			{in: "Cor_J", out: column{name: "core_energy_joule"}},
34			{in: "CorWatt", out: column{name: "core_power_watt"}},
35			{in: "UMHz1.0", out: column{name: "uncore_frequency_mhz_1_0"}},
36			{in: "Time_Of_Day_Seconds", out: column{name: "time_of_day_seconds", isTime: true}},
37		}
38		for _, tt := range tests {
39			t.Run(tt.in, func(t *testing.T) {
40				require.Equal(t, tt.out, createColumn(tt.in))
41			})
42		}
43	}
44	
45	func TestProcessValues(t *testing.T) {
46		columns := []column{
47			{name: "cpu", isTag: true},
48			{name: "core", isTag: true},
49			{name: "busy_frequency_mhz", isTag: false},
50			{name: "core_power_watt", isTag: false},
51		}
52		tests := []struct {
53			values []string
54			metric telegraf.Metric
55		}{
56			{
57				values: []string{"-", "-", "1.23", "4.56"},
58				metric: metric.New(
59					"turbostat",
60					map[string]string{"cpu": "-", "core": "-"},
61					map[string]interface{}{"busy_frequency_mhz": 1.23, "core_power_watt": 4.56},
62					time.Time{},
63				),
64			},
65			{
66				values: []string{"0", "1", "1.23"},
67				metric: metric.New(
68					"turbostat",
69					map[string]string{"cpu": "0", "core": "1"},
70					map[string]interface{}{"busy_frequency_mhz": 1.23},
71					time.Time{},
72				),
73			},
74		}
75		for _, tt := range tests {
76			t.Run(fmt.Sprintf("%s", tt.values), func(t *testing.T) {
77				acc := &testutil.Accumulator{}
78				err := processValues(acc, columns, tt.values)
79				require.NoError(t, err)
80				actual := acc.GetTelegrafMetrics()
81				testutil.RequireMetricsEqual(t, []telegraf.Metric{tt.metric}, actual, testutil.IgnoreTime())
82			})
83		}
84	}
85	
86	func TestProcessValuesErrors(t *testing.T) {
87		columns := []column{
88			{name: "cpu", isTag: true},
89			{name: "core", isTag: true},
90			{name: "busy_frequency_mhz", isTag: false},
91			{name: "core_power_watt", isTag: false},
92		}
93		tests := []struct {
94			values []string
95			err    string
96		}{
97			{
98				values: []string{"0", "1"},
99				err:    "no value for any field",
100			},
101			{
102				values: []string{"0", "1", "123", "456", "789"},
103				err:    "too many values: 4 columns, 5 values",
104			},
105			{
106				values: []string{"0", "1", "xyz"},
107				err:    "unable to parse column \"busy_frequency_mhz\": strconv.ParseFloat: parsing \"xyz\": invalid syntax",
108			},
109		}
110		for _, tt := range tests {
111			t.Run(fmt.Sprintf("%s", tt.values), func(t *testing.T) {
112				var acc testutil.Accumulator
113				err := processValues(&acc, columns, tt.values)
114				require.ErrorContains(t, err, tt.err)
115			})
116		}
117	}
118	
119	func TestCases(t *testing.T) {
120		dirs, err := os.ReadDir("testcases")
121		require.NoError(t, err)
122		for _, f := range dirs {
123			// Only handle directories.
124			if !f.IsDir() {
125				continue
126			}
127	
128			testcasePath := filepath.Join("testcases", f.Name())
129			configFilename := filepath.Join(testcasePath, "telegraf.conf")
130			inputFilename := filepath.Join(testcasePath, "input.txt")
131			expectedFilename := filepath.Join(testcasePath, "expected.out")
132	
133			t.Run(f.Name(), func(t *testing.T) {
134				// Load the expected output.
135				parser := &influx.Parser{}
136				require.NoError(t, parser.Init())
137				expected, err := testutil.ParseMetricsFromFile(expectedFilename, parser)
138				require.NoError(t, err)
139	
140				// Configure the plugin.
141				cfg := config.NewConfig()
142				require.NoError(t, cfg.LoadConfig(configFilename))
143				require.Len(t, cfg.Inputs, 1)
144				plugin := cfg.Inputs[0].Input.(*Turbostat)
145				require.NoError(t, plugin.Init())
146	
147				// Replace Turbostat with a mock process which outputs the desired lines.
148				exe, err := os.Executable()
149				require.NoError(t, err)
150				plugin.command = []string{exe, "-mockInput=" + inputFilename}
151	
152				// Start the plugin and wait for the expected metrics.
153				var acc testutil.Accumulator
154				require.NoError(t, plugin.Start(&acc))
155	
156				require.Eventually(t, func() bool {
157					return acc.NMetrics() == uint64(len(expected))
158				}, 3*time.Second, 100*time.Millisecond)
159	
160				require.Empty(t, acc.Errors)
161	
162				actual := acc.GetTelegrafMetrics()
163				testutil.RequireMetricsEqual(t, expected, actual, testutil.IgnoreTime())
164			})
165		}
166	}
167	
168	// This function lets the test executable to behave as a mock Turbostat process.
169	func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
170		var mockInput string
171		flag.StringVar(&mockInput, "mockInput", "", "mock turbostat output")
172		flag.Parse()
173	
174		if mockInput == "" {
175			os.Exit(m.Run())
176		}
177	
178		err := func() error {
179			file, err := os.Open(mockInput)
180			if err != nil {
181				return err
182			}
183			defer file.Close()
184	
185			_, err = io.Copy(os.Stdout, file)
186			if err != nil {
187				return err
188			}
189			return nil
190		}()
191		if err != nil {
192			os.Exit(5) // EIO
193		}
194	
195		// Sleep. Terminating immediately would look like Turbostat crashed.
196		time.Sleep(3 * time.Second)
197	}

content.text: I found the turbostat input implementation and tests. The parser currently treats any non-tag/non-time column as a float, so if turbostat emits a repeated or alternate header line containing the literal value `usec`, it returns the reported parse error. I'll inspect the existing fixture format next.

content.text:
Implemented the turbostat parser fix.

Summary:
- Identified the issue in `plugins/inputs/turbostat/turbostat.go`.
- The parser previously skipped repeated headers only when the raw line matched the first header exactly.
- Newer turbostat output can repeat the header with different spacing/alignment, causing `usec` to be parsed as a float value and producing:
  `unable to parse column "usec": strconv.ParseFloat: parsing "usec": invalid syntax`
- Updated column metadata to retain the original header token.
- Updated value processing to skip rows when a value matches its corresponding header token, independent of line spacing.
- Removed the fragile raw-line equality check.

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression test reproducing the repeated-header parsing case.
- Confirmed it failed before the fix.
- Applied the fix and confirmed the regression test passed.
- Removed the temporary regression test as requested.
- Ran the remaining turbostat tests successfully:

```text
go test ./plugins/inputs/turbostat -count=1

```
